name: Greenland (Kalaallit Nunaat / Grønland)
summary: Greenland (Kalaallit Nunaat) is a North Atlantic civil-law autonomous territory of the Kingdom of Denmark — structurally distinctive globally as the largest island state with Inuit-majority population operating under the 2009 Self-Government Act (Selvstyreloven) with high autonomy. Family-law framework operates under specially adapted Danish legal-system framework — the Greenlandic Family Law and Children Act (Inatsisartutlov om børn) with Inuit customary-law (Inuit-customary) elements. Greenland has independent legislative competence over family-law matters since the 2009 Self-Government Act. Parental responsibility (forældreansvar) and child custody are governed by Greenlandic Family Law and the Children Act. The High Court of Greenland (Grønlands Landsret) is the apex domestic court for civil and criminal matters; final appellate jurisdiction was retained with the Danish Supreme Court (Højesteret). Family-law matters are heard at first instance in the Court of Greenland (Retten i Grønland) — specialised circuit-court system covering Greenland's geographic dispersion. Psychology profession is regulated through the Greenlandic Healthcare Authority. Greenland is silent on 'parental alienation' as a statutory label; courts operate substantively under the best-interests-of-the-child standard. Greenland is a Hague Convention 1980 party via Danish territorial extension effective 1 July 1991.

statutory_framework.title: Greenlandic Family Law
statutory_framework.relevance: Greenlandic Family Law adapted to Greenlandic conditions governing marriage, parental responsibility, and child custody.

statutory_framework.title: Self-Government Act
statutory_framework.relevance: Federal Self-Government Act establishing Greenland's high autonomy with independent legislative competence over family-law matters.

apex_courts.name: High Court of Greenland (Grønlands Landsret)
apex_courts.role: Apex domestic appellate court for civil and criminal matters.

apex_courts.name: Danish Supreme Court (Højesteret)
apex_courts.role: Final appellate court for civil and criminal matters from Greenland.

professional_regulators.name: Greenlandic Healthcare Authority
professional_regulators.role: Federal regulator of health and allied health professionals including clinical psychology.

anonymisation_convention: Greenlandic family-court decisions are anonymised per High Court practice using initials.

key_developments.title: Danish colonisation + Hans Egede + Lutheran-mission framework
key_developments.description: Danish colonisation of Greenland commenced 1721 under Hans Egede Lutheran-missionary framework — substantively distinctive Arctic Danish-colonial framework. Subsequent 300+ years Danish-administration framework affecting subsequent constitutional-administrative-trajectory.

key_developments.title: Constitution-amendment + colonial-status-end + County-Denmark framework
key_developments.description: Constitutional amendment 5 June 1953 ending Greenland's colonial status — substantively distinctive Arctic decolonisation-via-constitutional-integration framework. Substantive County-of-Denmark status persisting through 1979 Home Rule affecting subsequent constitutional-democratic-trajectory.

key_developments.title: Home Rule established + Hjemmestyre framework
key_developments.description: Home Rule (Hjemmestyre) established 1 May 1979 within Kingdom of Denmark following 1979 Home Rule referendum (70.1% in favour). Substantively distinctive Arctic Inuit-self-governance framework. Foundational pre-2009-Self-Government-Act framework affecting subsequent autonomy-trajectory.

key_developments.title: Greenland EEC withdrawal + first-EEC-withdrawal-in-history framework
key_developments.description: Greenland withdrew from European Economic Community 1 February 1985 following 1982 referendum (52% in favour of withdrawal) — substantively distinctive globally first-EEC-withdrawal-in-history framework. Substantive cross-border-jurisdiction-practice framework affecting subsequent constitutional-democratic-trajectory.

key_developments.title: Hague Convention 1980 territorial extension + Denmark-UNCRC-Greenland-extension framework
key_developments.description: Hague Convention 1980 territorial extension by Denmark to Greenland effective 1 July 1991. Denmark ratified UNCRC 19 July 1991 with extension to Greenland — establishing barnets-bedste best-interests-of-the-child substantive doctrine within Greenlandic-civil-law framework.

key_developments.title: Hans Island dispute + Canada-Denmark-microconflict framework
key_developments.description: Hans Island dispute Canada-Denmark 1973-2022 — substantively distinctive globally micro-territorial-dispute-with-Whisky-War-cordial-exchange framework. Resolution 14 June 2022 with Canada-Denmark split-island framework. Substantive Arctic cross-border-jurisdiction-practice framework.

key_developments.title: Self-Government Act (Selvstyreloven) + Naalakkersuisut framework
key_developments.description: Federal Self-Government Act adopted 21 June 2009 establishing Greenland's high autonomy with independent legislative competence over family-law matters — substantively distinctive Arctic high-autonomy framework following 2008 self-government referendum (75.5% in favour). Naalakkersuisut (Greenland Government) framework affecting subsequent constitutional-democratic-trajectory.

key_developments.title: Greenlandic Family Law (Inatsisartutlov om familier)
key_developments.description: Greenlandic Family Law adapted to Greenlandic conditions governing marriage, parental responsibility, and child custody. Substantively significant Arctic Inuit-customary-element-integration framework affecting subsequent family-law-implementation framework. Substantive Inuit-customary-law-+-Danish-derivative-civil-law hybrid framework.

key_developments.title: Trump-Greenland-acquisition-rhetoric 2025 + geopolitical-attention framework
key_developments.description: Donald Trump renewed Greenland-acquisition-rhetoric January 2025 — substantively distinctive globally micro-territorial-acquisition-rhetoric-by-US-president framework. Substantive Arctic geopolitical-attention framework affecting subsequent constitutional-democratic-trajectory and cross-border-jurisdiction-practice. Subsequent Greenlandic election March 2025 substantively significant constitutional-democratic-trajectory.

key_developments.title: Egede premiership + Naalakkersuisut framework + IUI institutional framework
key_developments.description: Múte Bourup Egede (IA) premiership 2021-2025 framework consolidation through 2023. Naalakkersuisut (Government of Greenland) framework substantively significant institutional context. IUI (Inuit-language framework) substantively significant for Greenlandic-language family-law adjudication. Self-government framework substantively significant Arctic institutional context. Substantively significant Arctic Inuit institutional jurisprudential development.

key_developments.title: Grønlands Landsret + Højesteret — best-interests-of-the-child substantive register + Egede-presidency-continuing framework
key_developments.description: High Court of Greenland (Grønlands Landsret) and Danish Supreme Court (Højesteret) continue to develop best-interests-of-the-child jurisprudence under Greenlandic Family Law 2014 + Self-Government Act 2009 + Hague Convention 1980 framework in custody disputes within Múte Bourup Egede (IA) premiership since 23 April 2021. Substantive analysis without doctrinal 'parental alienation' label adoption.

structural_findings: Greenland operates a Danish-derivative civil-law framework with substantial Inuit customary-law adaptation — places Greenland in the Nordic civil-law cluster with structurally distinctive autonomous-territory + Inuit-customary-element framework.
Largest island state with Inuit-majority population is structurally distinctive globally.
High autonomy under 2009 Self-Government Act with independent legislative competence over family-law is structurally distinctive within Nordic/European framework.
Hague Convention 1980 applicability via Danish territorial extension reflects structurally distinctive autonomous-territory Hague jurisdiction status.
Bilingual official-language framework (Greenlandic + Danish) reflects post-1979 autonomy.
editorial_notes: Greenland jurisdiction sidecar v1.1 — deepened 2026-06-09 from 4 to 10 key_developments with full Danish-colonisation-to-Trump-rhetoric trajectory: 1721-Danish-colonisation-+-Hans-Egede + 1953-Constitution-amendment-+-colonial-status-end + 1979-Home-Rule-established-+-Hjemmestyre + 1985-Greenland-EEC-withdrawal + 1991-Hague-Convention-1980-+-UNCRC-extension + 1995-Hans-Island-dispute + 2009-Self-Government-Act-Selvstyreloven + 2014-Greenlandic-Family-Law + 2025-Trump-Greenland-acquisition-rhetoric + 2024-Grønlands-Landsret-+-Højesteret-+-Egede.

Civil-law autonomous-territory Nordic (Greenlandic Family Law 2014 + Self-Government Act 2009 + Inuit-customary-element + bilingual + Hague Convention via Danish territorial extension 1991). Largest island state with Inuit-majority population.

PA-recognition: silent statutory + no-apex-position + silent regulator — substantive best-interests-of-the-child analysis under Greenlandic Family Law 2014 + Self-Government Act 2009 + Inuit-customary-element framework without doctrinal 'parental alienation' label.

Joins Nordic + civil-law + autonomous-territory-with-independent-legislative-competence-over-family-law-distinctive + Inuit-customary-element + largest-island-state-with-Inuit-majority-population-globally-distinctive + Hans-Egede-Lutheran-colonisation-1721 + County-of-Denmark-1953-1979 + Home-Rule-Hjemmestyre-1979 + first-EEC-withdrawal-in-history-1985-globally-distinctive + Self-Government-Act-2009-75.5-percent-referendum + Hans-Island-dispute-+-Whisky-War-+-2022-resolution + Trump-Greenland-acquisition-rhetoric-2025 + Múte-Bourup-Egede-IA-premiership-2021 + bilingual-Greenlandic-Danish + Hague-via-Danish-territorial-extension clusters within the corpus.
